task: Write a Python function longest_word(s) returning the longest whitespace-separated word in the string. On ties return the last such word, not the first. An empty string returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code handles the empty string edge case correctly by returning an empty string.
trace: The `longest_word` function splits the input string into a list of words using whitespace as the delimiter. It then iterates through these words in reverse order, keeping track of the longest word found so far and updating it whenever a longer (or equal-length) word is encountered. This ensures that if there are multiple words with the same maximum length, the last one will be returned, adhering to the specified requirement.
